patches.summary: Add persistent --subdomain flag; client rewrites substack.com host to <sub>.substack.com for Creator endpoints.
patches.reason: Substack's API splits global endpoints (categories, publications search, /reader/feed, /subscriptions) from Creator endpoints (drafts, posts, notes, sections, dashboard). Creator endpoints live on the publication subdomain and return 404 when called against substack.com. Without per-request host rewriting, the entire Creator surface is unreachable.
patches.validated_outcome: Live test against three real publications: posts list, drafts CRUD, notes list, sections list all return 200 with --subdomain set; same calls 404 without it.

patches.summary: Route auth.type:cookie values to the Cookie HTTP header instead of Authorization, in both live and dry-run paths.
patches.reason: The generator's default places every authHeader value into Authorization:, but Substack's cookie auth requires Cookie: substack.sid=...; without this routing every authenticated request returns 401 "No auth token in header".
patches.validated_outcome: categories list, publications search, drafts list/create/update/delete/schedule, profiles me, me subscriptions all return 200 after the fix.

patches.summary: Markdown to Substack ProseMirror JSON converter for draft bodies (strong/em/code/link marks; heading, lists, blockquote; highlighted_code_block with language+nodeId; inline_latex/latex_block with persistentExpression+id; paywall; horizontal_rule).
patches.reason: Without this converter the generator's drafts create can only send raw strings, which the Substack editor rejects with a render-time error. Schema names were extracted from Substack's own editor JS bundle and verified by round-tripping every node type through the live editor.
patches.validated_outcome: Kitchen-sink draft (V13: marks + headings + lists + blockquote + code + LaTeX inline + LaTeX block + paywall) renders cleanly in the Substack editor UI.

patches.summary: drafts schedule <id> --at <time> command — POST /api/v1/drafts/{id}/scheduled_release with trigger_at + audience flags. Time inputs without an explicit timezone are parsed in time.Local.
patches.reason: Substack supports scheduled publication via this endpoint but the generator did not emit a command for it. Local-time parsing matches user expectation ("2026-06-01 09:00" means 9am in the user's tz, not UTC).
patches.validated_outcome: Live test: created throwaway draft, scheduled it for 2026-12-01T09:00:00Z, verified it appeared in /api/v1/post_management/scheduled with total=1, then deleted.

patches.summary: Rewrote drafts create and drafts update to expose 30+ Substack fields (title, subtitle, body via 3 input modes, audience, bylines auto-resolved, section, type, cover_image, SEO triple, comment-sort/perms, visibility, podcast/video fields).
patches.reason: Generator emitted only title/subtitle/body/audience/section-id. Substack's POST /api/v1/drafts accepts a much larger field set; without it agents can't author real posts (no SEO, no cover image, no paywall config, no podcast fields).

patches.summary: posts list --type filter (newsletter|podcast|video|thread). Substack uses a single posts endpoint with a type discriminator instead of separate APIs.
patches.reason: Live discovery showed Substack treats podcasts, videos, and chat threads as posts with type=*. The generator emitted a single posts list with no filter; adding the flag is the right way to expose all four post types.

patches.summary: Eight new commands that operate across multiple publications: portfolio, posts twin, posts best, posts pair/pairs, subscribers churn, subscribers cross-sell, grep, schedule board.
patches.reason: Substack's web UI is single-publication. Multi-publication workflows (cross-pub post duplication, subscriber-list joins for cross-sell, FTS over years of writing, churn diff over snapshots) have no Substack-side equivalent and have to be built on the local SQLite store.

patches.summary: Integrated seven API features from chirantan's PR #325 (v4.2.0): drafts prepublish, tags list/create, publications authors, posts archive, posts ranked-authors, profiles posts, profiles from-linkedin, comments get.
patches.reason: This PR replaces PR #325; importing its reachable API surface ensures no functional regression on features that worked there. drafts prepublish and profiles from-linkedin return 404/403 respectively against my live test as of 2026-05-14 and are documented as known gaps.

patches.summary: Completed .printing-press.json with run_id, printer, printer_name, category, novel_features, description after lock-promote left them blank.
patches.reason: The original promote step couldn't find research.json (state.RunID was empty) and wrote a minimal manifest. publish validate then failed on manifest/transcendence/phase5 checks until those fields were restored from the run's research.json and Phase 5 acceptance proof.

patches.summary: Restored the canonical SKILL.md install section so verify-skill's canonical-sections drift check passes.
patches.reason: SKILL.md initially shipped with the pre-publish install section ("public-library category not yet available"); once the manifest got a category, the verifier expected the post-publish form with the explicit go install path.
